name: Missouri
tagline: Back-to-back mega data centers — Google's $15B and Amazon's $10B, both in Montgomery County — plus a statewide AI framework due late 2026 and a consolidated central IT shop serving 15 executive departments make Missouri a national AI-infrastructure story under a cost-disciplined new administration.

execSummary.summary: Missouri enters FY2026 under first-term Governor Mike Kehoe with a $50.8B operating budget and an explicit message that 'spending, not revenue, is the problem' — meaning sellers should expect cost-justified, efficiency-framed buys rather than open-ended expansion. Three threads matter most. First, Google's $15B AI data-center campus in New Florence (announced May 2026) — backed by 1+ gigawatt of new generation — makes Missouri a national AI-infrastructure story and reshapes the energy and economic-development conversation. Second, Executive Order 26-02 (Jan 2026) directs the Office of Administration to deliver a statewide AI integration framework by November 30, 2026, opening the door to governed government AI. Third, the buying motion is centralized: OA-ITSD (~1,000 staff) runs IT for 15 consolidated executive departments, and the OA Division of Purchasing runs the statewide-contract and cooperative-procurement vehicles every deal flows through. For Dell, the play is AI-ready infrastructure, a client/endpoint refresh framed on efficiency, and getting onto (or validating coverage on) Missouri statewide contracts early.
opportunities: AI-infrastructure demand riding the Google New Florence buildout and statewide AI-framework momentum (PowerEdge, networking, services).
Governed, on-prem / private-cloud AI for agencies operationalizing the EO 26-02 framework on sensitive state data.
Endpoint, client, and lifecycle refresh across 15 OA-ITSD-consolidated departments, framed on the administration's efficiency mandate.
Cybersecurity modernization tied to SLCGP whole-of-state grants and OA-ITSD Information Security.

budget.intro: Missouri budgets on an annual fiscal-year cycle beginning July 1. Governor Kehoe signed the $50.8B FY2026 operating budget (about $15.4B in general revenue) on June 30, 2025, issuing more than 200 line-item vetoes to constrain spending. There is no single published 'state IT budget' line — technology spend is embedded across agencies and delivered centrally through OA-ITSD. The FY2027 cycle is shaped by a December 2025 consensus revenue estimate of $13.65B net general revenue (3.8% growth), with the administration signaling continued spending discipline.

breakdown.label: AI framework implementation
breakdown.note: No dedicated appropriation yet; framework due Nov 2026

notes: Missouri budgets annually (FY starts July 1) — figures are single-year unless noted.
Technology is funded through agency budgets and delivered by OA-ITSD; there is no consolidated public 'IT budget' line to forecast against.
Expect cost-justified, efficiency-framed buys under the Kehoe administration's 'spending is the problem' posture.

FY2027 (governor's proposed, per NASBO): $16.3B general revenue, +0.01% vs FY2026; $54.5B all funds, -1.1%. Fiscal-discipline 'Foundation for Growth' budget cutting $600M+ from core operations with a thin $4.7M ending balance.

initiatives.name: Google New Florence AI Data Center
initiatives.summary: Announced May 2026, Google's $15B campus on 900+ acres in New Florence (Montgomery County) is described as among the largest investments in Missouri history. Google has contracted 1+ gigawatt of new generation plus ~500MW through Ameren. It anchors Missouri as a national AI-infrastructure hub and is the strongest tailwind for Dell ISG / AI-factory and ecosystem demand in the state.

initiatives.name: Statewide AI Integration Framework (EO 26-02)
initiatives.summary: Executive Order 26-02 (signed Jan 15, 2026) directs OA Commissioner Kenneth Zellers to deliver a statewide AI integration framework by Nov 30, 2026 — prioritizing service delivery, data privacy/security, human-in-the-loop decisions, transparency, and data quality. The order also tasks DNR and the PSC with addressing data-center energy demand. Agencies will need governed, auditable AI — an opening for private/on-prem AI on sensitive workloads.

initiatives.name: OA-ITSD Consolidated IT Services
initiatives.summary: OA-ITSD (~1,000 professionals) is the consolidated IT provider for 15 executive departments plus the Governor's and Lt. Governor's offices, delivering client engagement, GIS, information security, networks/telecom, servers/storage/email, and app development. Consolidation concentrates infrastructure, endpoint, and lifecycle decisions in one buyer — a high-leverage relationship for Dell across server, storage, and client.

initiatives.name: Whole-of-State Cybersecurity (SLCGP)
initiatives.summary: Missouri's State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (administered by DPS/Office of Homeland Security, ~$1.9M FY2025 federal award) funds local-government planning, monitoring, protection, and legacy-tech upgrades — capped at $200K per applicant. Combined with OA-ITSD Information Security, this is a modest but steady channel for endpoint security, infrastructure hardening, and modernization in local jurisdictions.

initiatives.name: Connecting All Missourians Broadband (BEAD)
initiatives.summary: The DED Office of Broadband Development won NTIA approval (Jan 2026) to award $814M of Missouri's $1.7B BEAD allocation — reaching 200,000+ locations with ~81% fiber while saving ~$900M. Primarily a connectivity program rather than a direct Dell compute play, but it expands the addressable government and community-anchor footprint over time.

procurement.intro: Missouri's technology procurement is centralized under the Office of Administration. The OA Division of Purchasing competitively bids and maintains Statewide Contracts — 150+ categories and 650+ individual contracts — that all executive agencies use and that are extended to local governments and quasi-public bodies through the Cooperative Procurement Program. OA-ITSD is the consolidated IT delivery arm that operationalizes these buys for 15 executive departments.

vehicles.name: Missouri Statewide Contracts
vehicles.detail: Primary vehicle. Competitively bid (RFP) and re-bid by the OA Division of Purchasing; 150+ categories. Confirm Dell agreements and resellers are current and correctly scoped for target agencies.

vehicles.name: Cooperative Procurement Program
vehicles.detail: Extends statewide contracts to eligible local governments, political subdivisions, and quasi-public bodies. Voluntary participation — broadens reach beyond state agencies.

vehicles.name: NASPO ValuePoint / national cooperatives
vehicles.detail: Missouri participates in national cooperative agreements that can serve as alternate paths where a statewide contract gap exists. Validate the cleanest route per opportunity.

entities.name: OA Division of Purchasing
entities.role: Central procurement authority; owns statewide contracts and the cooperative program.

entities.name: OA-ITSD
entities.role: Consolidated IT provider for 15 executive departments; the operational technology buyer and standards owner.

entities.name: Dept. of Economic Development (DED)
entities.role: Office of Broadband Development; data-center / economic-development incentives tied to the Google project.

entities.name: University of Missouri System
entities.role: Independent research-computing and AI/informatics buyer (NextGen Precision Health, MU informatics).

procurement.competition: Missouri is a centralized, contract-driven, cost-conscious market. With buying concentrated in OA-ITSD and the Division of Purchasing, incumbents on statewide contracts hold an advantage, and the Kehoe administration's efficiency posture rewards clear ROI. Differentiation comes from AI-infrastructure capability tied to the data-center buildout, governed-AI readiness for the EO 26-02 framework, and lifecycle/TCO economics rather than price alone.

powerStructure.name: Mike Kehoe
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: First-term Republican; champions the Google data-center win and an efficiency-first budget — 'spending, not revenue, is the problem.'

powerStructure.name: Kenneth Zellers
powerStructure.title: Commissioner
powerStructure.note: Owns delivery of the statewide AI integration framework due Nov 30, 2026 under EO 26-02; oversees OA-ITSD and Purchasing.

powerStructure.name: John Laurent
powerStructure.title: State CIO
powerStructure.note: Appointed CIO Dec 2024; former Global VP of IT at Enterprise Holdings and Accenture consultant. Owns statewide IT direction and standards.

powerStructure.name: OA Division of Purchasing
powerStructure.title: Central procurement authority
powerStructure.note: Bids and maintains the statewide contracts and cooperative program every technology deal runs through.

news.title: Amazon to invest $10B in Montgomery County data-center campus
news.impact: A second AI-scale anchor in the same county as Google's New Florence project; deepens Missouri's AI-infrastructure ecosystem and the Dell ISG/partner tailwind.

news.title: Missouri General Assembly sends ~$50.7B FY2027 budget to Governor Kehoe
news.impact: Disciplined FY2027 plan reaches the governor's desk (signing deadline end of June); sellers should expect cost-justified, efficiency-led IT buys.

news.title: Google announces $15B AI data center in New Florence
news.impact: Largest-scale AI-infrastructure investment in state history; reshapes energy and economic-development agenda and creates a major ecosystem tailwind for Dell ISG.

news.title: Missouri AI regulation bills stall amid federal-funding concerns
news.impact: State AI rules remain unsettled as lawmakers weigh risk to rural broadband funds; governed-AI guidance now flows mainly through the executive branch.

news.title: Gov. Kehoe proposed a $16.3B general-revenue FY2027 budget
news.impact: $54.5B all funds, -1.1% vs FY2026; a fiscal-discipline budget cutting $600M+ from core operations with a thin $4.7M ending balance. Reinforces Missouri's commitment to safely integrate AI into state-government operations under the Missouri GREAT initiative.

news.title: Governor Kehoe signs Executive Order 26-02 on AI
news.impact: Mandates a statewide AI integration framework by Nov 30, 2026; agencies will need governed, auditable AI approaches.

news.title: NTIA approves Missouri's $814M BEAD broadband plan
news.impact: Funds fiber to 200,000+ locations while saving ~$900M of the $1.7B allocation; expands the long-term connected-government footprint.

dellActions: Lead with AI-ready infrastructure: tie Dell ISG / AI Factory to the Google New Florence buildout, the EO 26-02 framework, and University of Missouri research-computing demand.
Engage OA-ITSD as the consolidated buyer for 15 executive departments — server, storage, networking, and client/lifecycle, framed on the administration's efficiency mandate.
Validate Dell's coverage on Missouri statewide contracts and the cooperative program before pursuing deals; confirm resellers are current and correctly scoped.
Position governed, private/on-prem AI for sensitive state workloads ahead of the November 2026 AI framework deadline.
Map cybersecurity offerings to SLCGP whole-of-state grants and OA-ITSD Information Security for state-and-local endpoint and infrastructure modernization.
